In 1800, clear glass windows were a major luxury. The vast majority of the global population lived in rural, agricultural settings. Most dwellings had small openings covered by wooden shutters, oiled paper (common in parts of Asia), animal horn, or stretched membranes to keep out the cold and rain. None of these were transparent enough to casually "look through" without opening them—and opening them let out valuable heat. **3. Daily Life and Time Use:**
Over 80% of the world's population were agricultural workers. Their daylight hours were spent outdoors working the fields, tending to livestock, or doing manual labor.
